basicDetails.schemeName: Tailoring Training To Scheduled Caste & Backward Classes Widows/Destitute Women/Girls

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The aim of the scheme is to enable Scheduled Caste Widows/girls/destitute women for self-employment. A stipend of ₹600/- P.M. and raw material costing ₹300/- per month per trainee is given to a trainee for getting training from the nearest Kalyan Kendar run by this department. After completion of one year course, each widow/ girl/ destitute women are given a new sewing machine free of cost to earn her livelihood. 20 girls/ widows/ destitute women from SC community and 5 from BC community are given training in each community centre.
schemeContent.benefits_md:
- ₹600/- stipend per month.
- ₹300/- per month for raw materials.
- 1 sewing machine.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The aim of the scheme is to enable Scheduled Caste Widows/girls/destitute women for self-employment. A stipend of Rs.600/- P.M. and raw material costing Rs.300/- per month per trainee is given to a trainee for getting training from the nearest Kalyan Kendar run by this department.

children.text: The aim of the scheme is to enable Scheduled Caste Widows/girls/destitute women for self-employment. A stipend of ₹600/- P.M. and raw material costing ₹300/- per month per trainee is given to a trainee for getting training from the nearest Kalyan Kendar run by this department. After completion of one year course, each widow/ girl/ destitute women are given a new sewing machine free of cost to earn her livelihood. 20 girls/ widows/ destitute women from SC community and 5 from BC community are given training in each community centre.
₹600/- stipend per month.
₹300/- per month for raw materials.
1 sewing machine.

A trainee shall submit an application to the District Welfare Officer concerned through the Lady Social Worker concerned on a plain paper within the time limit as prescribed or notified by the Department from time to time. The application should be accompanied by a certificate signed by the member of the village Panch/ Sarpanch to this effect that the applicant is a Harijan Widow or a deserted/divorced woman.
Welfare of Scheduled Castes and Backward Classes Department
SCO 42-44, Sector 17 A, Chandigarh – 160017, Haryana.
Email: dbcharyana@gmail.com

applicationProcess.process_md:
A trainee shall submit an application to the District Welfare Officer concerned through the Lady Social Worker concerned on a plain paper within the time limit as prescribed or notified by the Department from time to time. The application should be accompanied by a certificate signed by the member of the village Panch/ Sarpanch to this effect that the applicant is a Harijan Widow or a deserted/divorced woman.
**Welfare of Scheduled Castes and Backward Classes Department**
SCO 42-44, Sector 17 A, Chandigarh – 160017, Haryana.
Tel: 01722704006
Email: dbcharyana@gmail.com

schemeDefinitions.name: Definition 1

children.text: “Government” means Administrative Department of the Haryana Government.
“Deserted or divorced women” means a woman who is not having conjugal rights with her husband for the first 2 years.

schemeDefinitions.definitions_md:
1. “Government” means Administrative Department of the Haryana Government.
1. “Deserted or divorced women” means a woman who is not having conjugal rights with her husband for the first 2 years.

schemeDefinitions.name: Definition 2

children.text: “Director” means the Director, Welfare of Scheduled Castes, and Backward Classes Haryan
“Committee” means the Committee consisting of District Welfare Officer and Lady Social Worker for the selection of candidates.

schemeDefinitions.definitions_md:
1. “Director” means the Director, Welfare of Scheduled Castes, and Backward Classes Haryan
1. “Committee” means the Committee consisting of District Welfare Officer and Lady Social Worker for the selection of candidates.

schemeDefinitions.name: Definition 3

children.text: “Centre” means Community Centres run by the Welfare of Scheduled Castes and Backward Classes Department Haryana.
“Head of Institution” means the Lady Social Worker of the Institution working in the Community Centre.

schemeDefinitions.definitions_md:
1. “Centre” means Community Centres run by the Welfare of Scheduled Castes and Backward Classes Department Haryana.
1. “Head of Institution” means the Lady Social Worker of the Institution working in the Community Centre.

schemeDefinitions.name: Definition 4

children.text: “Trainee” means Scheduled Caste widow and divorced or destitute women under training in the Community Centre.
“Widow” means Scheduled Caste women husband is not alive and she has not remarried

schemeDefinitions.definitions_md:
1. “Trainee” means Scheduled Caste widow and divorced or destitute women under training in the Community Centre.
1. “Widow” means Scheduled Caste women husband is not alive and she has not remarried

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md:
1.	The applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana.
2.	The applicant should be a widow, destitute woman, or girl.
3.	The applicant&#39;s age should be between 14 and 45 years.
4.	The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste or Backward Class category.
5.	The applicant&#39;s annual family income should be less than ₹2.5 lakhs.

children.text: 1.	The applicant should be a permanent resident of Haryana.
2.	The applicant should be a widow, destitute woman, or girl.
3.	The applicant's age should be between 14 and 45 years.
4.	The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste or Backward Class category.
5.	The applicant's annual family income should be less than ₹2.5 lakhs.
